meta_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve No Portfolio (Checklist)
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no portfolio' trap by mastering Git with AI. This intermediate guide uses Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional-grade projects.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is a myth solved by high-velocity development. By integrating AI-assisted version control with Antigravity and Claude Code, you can iterate faster, maintain clean commit history, and document complex architectural decisions automatically. Transform your raw coding experiments into a polished, professional showcase that proves your engineering competence to employers.
content.intro:
You stare at the blinking cursor, the weight of an empty GitHub profile pressing down on your ambitions. The industry demands experience, but you are trapped in the catch-22 of needing a job to get experience and experience to get a job. It is a quiet, brutal cycle that kills more careers before they start.

But here is the secret: engineering isn't just about writing code; it's about managing complexity and velocity. By mastering the synergy between Git, Antigravity, and Claude Code, you stop being a student who 'writes scripts' and become an architect who 'builds systems.' It’s time to turn your learning curve into a launchpad.

steps.title: Architecting the Repository Structure
steps.description: Establish a professional foundation by defining your project structure before writing logic. This ensures scalability and clean Git history.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a professional project directory structure for a SaaS MVP using a modern stack. Include folders for /src, /tests, /docs, and /config. Generate a README.md template that highlights technical challenges and architectural decisions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'initialize project structure with standard best practices'

steps.title: Automating Commit Semantics
steps.description: Stop committing with 'fixed stuff'. Use AI to enforce Conventional Commits, which makes your history readable for hiring managers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my staged changes and suggest a Conventional Commit message that explains the 'why' behind the code, not just the 'what'.

steps.title: Documenting Architectural Decisions
steps.description: Turn your thought process into ADRs (Architecture Decision Records). This demonstrates senior-level thinking to recruiters.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft an ADR for the current database schema choice. Focus on trade-offs regarding latency, scalability, and ease of migration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a new ADR file in /docs/adr/ explaining the current implementation strategy'

steps.title: AI-Powered Code Review Simulation
steps.description: Before pushing to main, have your AI agent review your code for security vulnerabilities and performance bottlenecks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Staff Engineer. Review the current codebase for memory leaks, inefficient loops, and security vulnerabilities. Suggest 3 specific optimizations.

steps.title: Portfolio-Ready README Deployment
steps.description: Your README is your landing page. Use AI to extract your development narrative into a compelling project summary.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a README section titled 'Engineering Challenges Overcome' based on the commit history and ADRs in this repo. Make it sound professional and results-oriented.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'update README.md with project highlights and technical achievements'

pro_tips: Always reference your Jira-style issue numbers in commit messages to show you can work in an Agile environment.
Use Git hooks to trigger AI linting before every push; it catches errors before they hit your remote history.
Structure your commit history to tell a story: start with the problem, show the experimentation, and end with the optimized solution.
Include a 'Performance Metrics' section in your README to provide hard data on how your code improved over time.
Don't just upload code; include a 'Lessons Learned' file that details what you would refactor if you had more time.
content.next_steps: After completing this, deploy your project to Vercel or AWS, link the live demo in your README, and pin the repository to your GitHub profile header.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical workflow to turn 'no experience' into 'proven results.' Don't let your code sit in a folder—ship it, document it, and own your growth. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
